page: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 3840 GB NVMe SSD.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong computational capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3697.29 places it in the top tier (top 10%), while the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 117958.63 is rated as strong (top 25%). The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES, VAES, SHA_NI), which optimize vector math and secure data processing.

Because the CPU allocation is shared, users may experience variable performance under sustained peak loads compared to dedicated instances. However, this shared model provides a highly cost-effective profile for memory-intensive workloads that require massive RAM density and fast local NVMe storage without the premium cost of dedicated hardware. This server is well-suited for large-scale databases, memory-cached applications, data analytics pipelines, and enterprise workloads requiring substantial memory capacity and fast local storage.
description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and includes 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance (top 25%) in stress-ng tests. The CPU supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and VAES. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model, this instance offers a cost-efficient alternative for resource-dense workloads. It is highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and large-scale data processing tasks that benefit from high memory capacity and fast local storage.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.

tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and fast local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.

512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
